book: Class 07 - English - An Alien Hand - Revised

questions.question_text: Whom does Golu ask, Why don't you ever fly like other birds?
questions.question_html: Whom does Golu ask, Why don’t you ever fly like other birds?

solutions.text: Golu asked his tall aunt, the ostrich, "Why don't you ever fly like other birds?"
solutions.html: Golu asked his tall aunt, the ostrich, "Why don’t you ever fly like other birds?"

questions.question_text: Which uncle of Golu had red eyes?
questions.question_html: Which uncle of Golu had red eyes?

solutions.text: Golu's huge uncle, the hippopotamus had red eyes.
solutions.html: Golu’s huge uncle, the hippopotamus had red eyes.

questions.question_text:
Golu's relatives did not answer his questions because:
they were shy.
the questions were too difficult.
Golu was a naughty baby.
questions.question_html:
Golu’s relatives did not answer his questions because:

  1. they were shy.
  2. the questions were too difficult.
  3. Golu was a naughty baby.

questions.question_text: Who advised Golu to go to the Limpopo river?
questions.question_html: Who advised Golu to go to the Limpopo river?

solutions.text: The mynah bird advised Golu to go to the Limpopo river.
solutions.html: The mynah bird advised Golu to go to the Limpopo river.

questions.question_text: Why did Golu go to the river?
questions.question_html: Why did Golu go to the river?

solutions.text: Golu went to the river to know what the crocodile had for his dinner.
solutions.html: Golu went to the river to know what the crocodile had for his dinner.

questions.question_text:
The crocodile lay on the bank of the Limpopo river. Golu thought it was:
a living crocodile.
a dead crocodile.
a log of wood.
questions.question_html:
The crocodile lay on the bank of the Limpopo river. Golu thought it was:

  1. a living crocodile.
  2. a dead crocodile.
  3. a log of wood.

questions.question_text: What did the crocodile do to show that it was a real crocodile?
questions.question_html: What did the crocodile do to show that it was a real crocodile?

solutions.text: Crocodile shed his crocodile tears to show that it was a real crocodile.
solutions.html: Crocodile shed his crocodile tears to show that it was a real crocodile.

questions.question_text:
Come here, little one, and I'll whisper the answer to you. The crocodile said this because:
he couldn't stand up.
he wanted to eat Golu.
Golu was deaf.
questions.question_html:
Come here, little one, and I’ll whisper the answer to you. The crocodile said this because:

  1. he couldn’t stand up.
  2. he wanted to eat Golu.
  3. Golu was deaf.

questions.question_text: Who helped Golu on the bank of the river?
questions.question_html: Who helped Golu on the bank of the river?

solutions.text: The python helped Golu on the river bank. He saved Golu's life from the crocodile.
solutions.html: The python helped Golu on the river bank. He saved Golu’s life from the crocodile.

questions.question_text: Name two things the elephant can do with his trunk, and two he cannot. [Golu Grows a Nose]
questions.question_html: Name two things the elephant can do with his trunk, and two he cannot. [Golu Grows a Nose]

solutions.text:
The two things the elephant can do with his trunk are:
pluck a large bundle of grass and take it into his mouth
scoop up some mud from the bank and slap it on his head.
The two things the elephant cannot do with his trunk are:
cannot scratch himself with his trunk
cannot chew or eat food directly with his trunk.
solutions.html:
The two things the elephant can do with his trunk are:

  1. pluck a large bundle of grass and take it into his mouth
  2. scoop up some mud from the bank and slap it on his head.

The two things the elephant cannot do with his trunk are:

  1. cannot scratch himself with his trunk
  2. cannot chew or eat food directly with his trunk.
